title: Money for Nothing - Synth Atmosphere Opening
characteristic: wide stereo opening with synth pad
description: Sting's processed 'I want my MTV' floats wide across the stereo field, demonstrating soundstage width before any rhythm enters. The synth tail decays into perceptible 'air' that reveals your headphones' high-frequency extension.

title: Money for Nothing - Knopfler Guitar Imaging
characteristic: precise stereo placement of two guitars
description: Knopfler's iconic riff sits slightly left of center while a second guitar layer hangs right. With no rhythm section to mask, you can pinpoint each guitar's exact stereo position - a textbook imaging demo.

title: Money for Nothing - Macro-Dynamic Band Entry
characteristic: explosive band entry after sparse intro
description: After 55 seconds of two guitars, the full band crashes in - drums, bass, everything at once. The macro-dynamic contrast between sparse intro and wall-of-sound chorus is reference material.

title: Aja - Fender Rhodes Tonal Color
characteristic: glassy bell-like Rhodes piano timbre
description: The Fender Rhodes electric piano shimmers with a glassy, bell-like quality. On revealing headphones you can hear the tine mechanism that gives the Rhodes its unique voice - a perfect timbre reference.

title: Aja - Steve Gadd Snare Detail
characteristic: snare drum micro-detail and ghost notes
description: Steve Gadd's legendary drum solo. Listen for the stick hitting different parts of the snare - center vs rim, the ghost notes between main hits. Each strike has unique texture and attack.

title: Aja - Multi-Instrument Separation
characteristic: follow individual instruments in dense mix
description: Piano, guitar, bass, drums and sax play simultaneously. Try following just the bass line, then re-listen following the piano. Great separation lets you isolate any instrument at will.

title: The Chain - Whisper-Quiet Opening
characteristic: deliberately low-volume intimate intro
description: The song opens delicately with soft finger-picked guitar and gentle vocals at unusually low volume. This deliberate restraint sets up the dynamic range to come - resist turning it up.

title: The Chain - John McVie Bass Riff
characteristic: iconic electric bass with body and texture
description: McVie's iconic bass riff after the breakdown. You can feel the roundness of each note, the slight fret buzz, the way the string vibrates - definition not just rumble.

title: Don't Know Why - Piano Air & Decay
characteristic: natural decay between piano chords
description: The piano opens alone. Listen to the space AROUND the notes - between the decay of one chord and the attack of the next. That ambient tail is 'air' in the recording.

title: Don't Know Why - Vocal & Brush Imaging
characteristic: centered vocal with brushed snare placement
description: Norah's voice enters dead center, slightly forward in the mix. The piano sits behind and slightly right; brushes on snare have a precise position. A textbook intimate-jazz image.

title: Giorgio by Moroder - Snare Transient Snap
characteristic: sharp programmed snare attack
description: The drums kick in with a SNAP that defines transient response. The hi-hat has metallic sizzle. A modern reference for drum attack speed.

title: Giorgio by Moroder - Bass Guitar Pitch Definition
characteristic: tight bass guitar with melodic definition
description: Bass guitar locks with kick drum. Each bass note has distinct pitch - you can hear the melody being played, not just rhythm. Tests low-end articulation.

title: Let Down - Three-Guitar Layering
characteristic: interlocking guitars at different tempos
description: Three guitars play interlocking patterns at different tempos - one left, one right, one center-ish. Each occupies its own depth slot. Tests the front-to-back resolution of your gear.

title: Let Down - Reverb-Soaked Outro Air
characteristic: manor house reverb decay
description: The song decays into reverb and fading guitars. The room sound enveloping the ending is the actual Manor recording space - a synthetic-feeling room captured naturally.

title: Hallelujah - Buckley Vocal Detail
characteristic: intimate vocal with breath and mouth detail
description: Buckley's voice enters softly. Listen for breathiness at phrase starts, voice cracks of emotion, mouth-shape changes. Reveals the intimacy a great vocal mic can capture.

title: Hallelujah - Whisper-to-Cry Dynamic Build
characteristic: huge vocal dynamic swing
description: Buckley builds from a whisper to a soaring cry. Don't adjust your volume - let the dynamic range work. The contrast tests headroom and your gear's quiet-to-loud linearity.

title: Teardrop - Sub-Bass Extension
characteristic: felt-not-heard sub-bass below 60Hz
description: A sub-bass pulse enters almost subliminally. You may feel it more than hear it. This is bass extension - sound below 60Hz that small drivers cannot reproduce.

title: Teardrop - Reverb-Floating Vocal
characteristic: vocal floating above instruments in reverb
description: Elizabeth Fraser's vocal floats ABOVE the instruments, swimming in reverb - not dry and present like Buckley but ethereal. A reference for synthetic reverb 'air'.

title: Teardrop - Five-Layer Vertical Stack
characteristic: depth-stacked sub, kick, harpsichord, percussion, vocal
description: Count the layers: sub-bass (deepest), kick drum, harpsichord (mid-depth), percussion (slightly forward), vocal (floating above). A vertical depth-test.

title: So What - Trumpet Brass-Buzz Transients
characteristic: trumpet attack: burst of air then brass tone
description: When Miles enters, notice the initial attack of each note - the burst of air, the brass buzz. Then the note settles. Tests transient leading-edge resolution.

title: So What - 30th Street Studio Air
characteristic: natural reverb of converted-church studio
description: The 30th Street Studio was a converted church with a high ceiling. You can HEAR that room in the natural reverb around each instrument - a benchmark for acoustic space.

title: So What - Quintet Stereo Imaging Map
characteristic: fixed positions for each horn
description: Miles is center-left, Coltrane center-right, Cannonball further right. The horns never wander - their placement is fixed and pinpointable. Classic 1959 stereo imaging.

title: Beethoven 7 Allegretto - Orchestral Soundstage
characteristic: Musikverein hall feel, low strings spread wide
description: The movement opens with low strings playing the famous theme. The soundstage feels ENORMOUS compared to pop recordings - the Musikverein's geometry captured in stereo.

title: Beethoven 7 Allegretto - Pianissimo to Forte Build
characteristic: orchestral dynamic range from pp to f
description: The theme builds from pianissimo to forte with utter control. An orchestra can go from near-silence to overwhelming power - this passage tests dynamic linearity end-to-end.

title: Beethoven 7 Allegretto - String Section Timbre
characteristic: violins silvery, violas dark, cellos resonant
description: Listen to the difference between string sections: violins are bright and silvery, violas darker and throatier, cellos warm and resonant. A masterclass in instrument timbre.

title: Roygbiv - Hip-Hop Break Transients
album: Music Has the Right to Children
characteristic: processed but crisp hi-hat sizzle
description: The beat enters with a hip-hop drum break. The hi-hat is processed but retains its sharp attack - shows transients can survive heavy effects chains.

title: Roygbiv - Detuned Bass Beating
album: Music Has the Right to Children
characteristic: warm analog bass with audible detune beating
description: A warm bass synth enters, detuned slightly. On good headphones you can hear the detuning as a gentle beating between two oscillators - tests low-frequency texture resolution.

title: Diana Krall - Olympia Theatre Air
track: I Love Being Here With You
characteristic: audience and theatre-room reverberation
description: Before the first note, listen to the room itself - the Olympia Theatre breathing through the recording. You'll hear audience and the natural room signature.

title: Diana Krall - Live Jazz Micro-Dynamics
track: I Love Being Here With You
characteristic: small accents typically compressed away
description: Live jazz lives in micro-dynamics - the small accents and decays that get compressed out of studio polish. Krall's piano touch reveals what your gear preserves.

title: N.Y. State of Mind - DJ Premier Snare Crack
characteristic: boom-bap razor-sharp drum programming
description: DJ Premier's drums drop in with razor-sharp transients - the snare cracks with authority over a deep kick. Reference golden-age hip-hop drum attack.

title: N.Y. State of Mind - Layered Sample Mix
characteristic: vocal, piano loop, trumpet stab, drums in distinct planes
description: Count the layers under Nas's voice: looped piano sample, jazz trumpet stab, drum break, bass. Each occupies its own depth slot - a sampling-era layering benchmark.

title: Pink Moon - Intimate Studio Air
characteristic: small warm room around a single guitar
description: The guitar enters alone. You immediately hear the small, warm studio space around the instrument - the air between notes is part of the recording.

title: Pink Moon - Voice Proximity Detail
characteristic: close-miked vocal with breath and proximity
description: Drake's voice enters intimately. Listen for the slight intake of breath before phrases and the proximity-effect bass on a close-miked vocal.

title: Fields of Gold - Voice Whisper-to-Swell
characteristic: vocal range from breathy to full-voiced inside one phrase
description: Cassidy drops to a near-whisper then swells to full power within a single phrase. Tests both dynamic range and timbral shifts as her voice changes character.

title: Xtal - Reverb-Wash Synthetic Air
characteristic: enormous synthetic reverb space
description: A wash of reverb-drenched pads opens the track, creating an enormous sense of space. This is synthetic air - reverb and delay used to manufacture room feel.

title: Xtal - Ambient Layer Counting
characteristic: pad, bass pulse, arpeggio, found-sound textures
description: Count the layers: ambient pad furthest back, bass pulse low and wide, mid-frequency synth arpeggio, scattered percussion. Each at its own depth.

title: Waltz for Debby - Village Vanguard Air
characteristic: audience murmurs and clinking glasses in club ambience
description: Between phrases, listen for the club - audience murmurs, clinking glasses, the room's tonal signature. Not noise but ambient evidence of the live space.

title: Waltz for Debby - LaFaro Bass Detail
characteristic: fingers on thick upright strings
description: Focus on LaFaro's bass technique - you can hear his fingers plucking the thick strings, the wood resonance of the instrument body. Acoustic bass detail benchmark.

title: Lateralus - Danny Carey Drum Attack
characteristic: tom and ride articulation in dense rock mix
description: Carey's drumming reveals each hit's attack - the crack of the snare, sharp ping of the ride. Even in a dense rock mix every drum strike is articulate.

title: Lateralus - Distorted Bass with Pitch
characteristic: growling distorted bass with note definition
description: Chancellor's bass has a distinctive growling, distorted tone yet the notes remain clearly defined. Tests whether your gear preserves pitch under low-frequency distortion.

title: A Case of You - Dulcimer Tonal Character
characteristic: Appalachian dulcimer nasal drone
description: Mitchell plays the Appalachian dulcimer - a rare instrument with a distinctive nasal, droning quality. A timbre that is unlike any guitar - tests your ear for unfamiliar instruments.

title: Untitled (How Does It Feel) - Pino Bass Tone
characteristic: warm round bass with full harmonic envelope
description: Palladino's bass guitar enters with extraordinary warmth and roundness. Each note has a full harmonic envelope - body, sustain, and decay all present.

title: Untitled (How Does It Feel) - Voice Stacks
characteristic: multi-tracked vocals each with different timbre
description: D'Angelo's multi-tracked vocals form a choir of himself. Each layer has slightly different timbre - some breathy, some full, some falsetto. A study in vocal stacking.

title: Samskeyti - Glacial Layer Build
characteristic: single keyboard tone joined gradually by others
description: A single keyboard tone sustains, joined gradually by a second voice, then a third. Each enters at a different pitch and depth - a slow-motion layering reveal.

title: Samskeyti - Slow-Motion Crescendo
characteristic: massive sustained orchestral swell
description: The climax arrives - a massive swell of orchestral and electronic forces. The dynamic distance from the opening sustained tone to this is enormous.

title: Jamming - Reggae Mix Separation
characteristic: reggae instruments in distinct stereo pockets
description: Reggae mixing puts each element in its own pocket: bass center-low, drums center, skank guitar hard left or right, organ on the opposite side. Reference instrument separation.

title: Hurt - Cash Voice Tremor Detail
album: American IV: The Man Comes Around
characteristic: age, tremor and waver in close-miked vocal
description: Cash's voice enters and the detail is extraordinary - the tremor of age, the slight waver, the edge of breath behind every word. A devastating proximity recording.

title: Hurt - Quiet-to-Climactic Build
album: American IV: The Man Comes Around
characteristic: near-spoken to strained climax then collapse
description: The song builds to its most intense moment - Cash's voice strains on 'empire of dirt.' Then everything falls away. A masterclass in dynamic-arc storytelling.

title: Poinciana - Tube-Chain Piano Warmth
album: At the Pershing: But Not for Me
characteristic: 1958 tube-recorded piano warmth and round attack
description: Jamal's piano has a warmth and roundness distinctly different from modern digital. The tube signal chain leaves a euphonic harmonic signature on every note.

title: 21st Century Schizoid Man - Vinyl Tonal Color
album: In the Court of the Crimson King
characteristic: warm vinyl-mastered overdriven ensemble
description: The distorted, overdriven ensemble has a specific character through vinyl mastering - slightly warmer and rounder than the digital remaster. A vinyl-vs-digital tonal showcase.

title: Bubbles - Object-Audio Imaging
characteristic: individual water drops at discrete head positions
description: Individual water drops are placed at specific positions around your head - some left, some right, some seemingly behind. A psychoacoustic imaging test.

title: Bubbles - Dense Element Separation
characteristic: dozens of placed sound elements simultaneously
description: Dozens of individual sound elements play simultaneously, each at its own position. Despite the density you can track any one - extreme separation reference.

title: Nardis - Green Mill Air
characteristic: long narrow low-ceiling club acoustic
description: The Green Mill's acoustic is present in every moment. Long, narrow, low-ceiling - reflections give the recording its specific spatial signature.

title: Nardis - Holographic Imaging
characteristic: instruments placed with surgical precision
description: Instruments appear with holographic precision. Barber's piano is placed with exact specificity - you can almost sense the wood-and-strings dimensions.

title: Heart of Gold - Hi-Res Soundstage Width
characteristic: Nashville session band wide warm stage
description: The Nashville musicians (Ben Keith on steel guitar) create a warm, wide soundstage. Listen for steel guitar that extends well beyond the speakers.
